name: Anguish of the Unredeemed

src.description: Each time a model in this unit is destroyed by a melee attack, if that model has not fought this phase, roll one D6. On a 2+, do not remove it from play; that destroyed model can fight after the attacking unit has finished making its attacks, and is then removed from play.

note: Missing roll requirement (2+), probability condition, and fighting rules.

name: Anchorite Sarcophagus

src.description: The bearer has a Move characteristic of 7"and a Save characteristic of 3+.

note: Incomplete; omits Save characteristic change entirely.

name: Deep Strike

src.description: Unit can be set up in Reserves instead of on the battlefield. Unit can be set up in your Reinforcements step, more than 9" horizontally away from all enemy models.

note: Omits minimum distance requirement (9") from enemies.

name: Leader

src.description: Before the battle, CHARACTER units with the Leader ability can be attached to one of their Bodyguard units to form an Attached unit.

note: Vague; omits timing (before battle) and specific attachment eligibility.

name: Divine Deliverance

src.description: Once per battle, at the start of the Fight phase, this model can use this ability. If it does, until the end of the phase, add 3 to the Attacks characteristic of melee weapons equipped by this model and those weapons have the [DEVASTATING WOUNDS] ability.

note: Omits 'once per battle' timing and self-only condition.

name: Rapturous Blows

src.description: Each time this model’s unit is selected to fight, you can discard 1 Miracle dice. If you do, until the end of the phase, each time a melee attack made by this model scores a wound, the target of that attack suffers 1 mortal wound in addition to any normal damage.

note: Omits Miracle dice cost, units vs model scope, and wound-dependent trigger.

name: Cherub

src.description:
Once per battle, after this unit has performed an Act of Faith, you gain 1 Miracle dice.

Designer’s Note: Place a Cherub token next to the unit, removing it once this ability has been used.

note: Shows '?' instead of actual value; Designer's note is flavor, not mechanics.

name: Salvationist Medikit

src.description: In your Command phase, if the bearer is on the battlefield, you can return up to D3 destroyed models (excluding CHARACTER models) to this unit.

note: Omits command phase timing and CHARACTER exclusion condition.

name: Simulacrum Imperialis

src.description: At the end of your Command phase, for each objective marker you control that has one or more units from your army with this ability within range of it, roll one D6:on a 4+, you gain 1 Miracle dice showing a value equal to that result.

note: Awkward phrasing; unclear if roll result equals the dice value.

name: Attached Unit

src.description: If a MINISTORUM PRIEST from your army with the Leader ability can be attached to a BATTLE SISTERS SQUAD, it can be attached to this unit instead. If a MINISTORUM PRIEST from your army is attached to this unit during the Declare Battle Formations step, that model gains the Scouts 6"ability.

note: Too vague; omits specific MINISTORUM PRIEST eligibility and Scouts tie-in.

name: Atttached Unit

src.description: If a MINISTORUM PRIEST or INQUISITOR model from your army with the Leader ability can be attached to a SISTERS OF BATTLE SQUAD, it can be attached to this unit instead. If a MINISTORUM PRIEST or INQUISITOR model from your army is attached to this unit during the Declare Battle Formations step, that model gains the Scouts 6"ability.

note: Too vague; omits INQUISITOR option and Scouts consequence.

name: Rituale Nullificatus

src.description: Models in this unit have the Feel No Pain 4+ ability against Psychic Attacks and mortal wounds.

note: Omits scope restriction (Psychic Attacks and mortal wounds only).

name: Virtue of Intolerance

src.description: At the start of the battle, select one unit from your opponent’s army to be this unit’s quarry. Each time a model in this unit makes an attack that targets its quarry, that attack has the [PRECISION] ability and you can re-roll the Hit roll. This ability can be used even if this unit is embarked within a TRANSPORT.

note: Omits quarry selection mechanic and EMBARKED exception.

name: Litany of Deeds

src.description: Each time you gain a Miracle dice as the result of an ADEPTA SORORITAS unit or model from your army being destroyed, if that unit or model was destroyed within 12"of this model, you can re-roll the result of that Miracle dice before adding it to your Miracle Dice pool.

note: Scope and condition phrasing confusing; unclear about trigger.

name: Stanchion of Holy Martyrs

src.description: While this model is leading a unit, models in that unit have a Save characteristic of 2+ and a 4+ invulnerable save.

note: Omits invulnerable save (4+) entirely.

name: Relics of the Matriarchs

src.description: At the start of the battle round, select up to two of the abilities in the Relics of the Matriarchs section. Until the start of the next battle round, this model has those abilities.

note: Vague; doesn't explain what relics are or how selection works.

name: Solemn Procession

src.description: Each time you gain 1 Miracle dice at the start of the battle round, if this model is on the battlefield, do not roll one D6 to determine the value of that Miracle dice; it has a value of 6.

note: Unclear grammar; 'fixed 6 override' not standard term.

name: Angelic Ascent

src.description: In your Shooting phase, after this unit has shot, if it is not within Engagement Range of any enemy units, it can make a Normal move of up to 6". If it does, until the end of the turn, this unit is not eligible to declare a charge.

note: Omits engagement range check and charge-restriction consequence.

name: Overseer of Redemption

src.description: While this unit contains a Repentia Superior model, each time a Sisters Repentia model in this unit makes a melee attack, you can re-roll the Hit roll and you can re-roll the Wound roll.

note: Omits Repentia Superior presence requirement and Sisters Repentia scope.

name: Laud Hailer

src.description: Once per battle, at the start of any phase, you can select one friendly ADEPTA SORORITAS unit that is Battle-shocked and within 12"of this model. That unit is no longer Battle-shocked.

note: Omits battle-shocked condition specificity; scope says aura but applies to units.

name: Purge and Cleanse

src.description: In your Shooting phase, after this model has shot, select one enemy unit hit by one or more of those attacks. Until the end of the phase, that enemy unit cannot have the Benefit of Cover.

note: Opposite mechanic; grants cover instead of denying it.

name: Sacred Healing

src.description: While this model is leading a unit, in your Command phase, you can return up to 1 destroyed model (excluding CHARACTER models) to that unit. If you wish, you can first discard 1 Miracle dice; if you do, you can return up to D3+1 destroyed models (excluding CHARACTER models) to that unit instead.

note: Omits Miracle dice discount option (D3+1 upgrade path).

name: Righteous Repugnance

src.description: Each time this model’s unit is selected to shoot or fight, you can discard 1 Miracle dice. If you do, until the end of the phase, add 3 to the Attacks characteristic of Fidelis and the Lance of Illumination. Each time an enemy unit is destroyed by this model, you gain 1 Miracle dice.

note: Omits Miracle dice cost, weapon specificity, and destroyed-enemy effect.

name: Cherubs

src.description:
Twice per battle, after this unit has performed an Act of Faith, you gain 1 Miracle dice.

Designer’s Note: Place two Cherub tokens next to the unit, removing one each time this ability has been used.

note: Says 'flavor text' instead of describing Miracle dice gain mechanic.

name: Storm of Retribution

src.description: Each time a model in this unit makes a ranged attack, re‐roll a Hit roll of 1 and re‐roll a Wound roll of 1. If such an attack targets an enemy unit that has destroyed one or more ADEPTA SORORITAS units from your army during the battle, add 1 to the Hit roll and add 1 to the Wound roll as well.

note: Omits condition (destroyed units) for bonus; phrasing suggests always on.

name: Impetuous Fervour

src.description: Each time a model in this unit makes an attack, re-roll a Hit roll of 1. If the target of that attack is an enemy unit within range of an objective marker, you can re-roll the Hit roll instead.

note: Omits conditional re-roll (only near objectives) and full mechanism.

name: Sacred Banner

src.description: You can re-roll Advance and Charge rolls made for the bearer’s unit.

note: Includes Advance rolls but generated only mentions Charge.

name: Lone Operative

src.description: Unless part of an Attached unit, this unit can only be selected as the target of a ranged attack if the attacking model is within 12".

note: Omits 'Attached unit' exception and visibility requirement.

name: Holy Judgement

src.description: At the start of your Shooting phase, select one enemy unit within 12"of and visible to this unit’s Ephrael Stern model. That unit must take a Battle-shock test, subtracting 2 from the result if it is a CHAOS unit. If the test is failed, that enemy unit suffers 3 mortal wounds.

note: Omits battle-shock test; says direct mortal wounds instead.

name: Mysterious Saviours

src.description: You can target this unit with the Heroic Intervention Stratagem for 0CP, and can do so even if you have already targeted a different unit with that Stratagem this phase.

note: Shows '?' instead of value (0CP); omits stratagem mechanics.

name: Self Repair

src.description: At the start of your Command phase, this model regains 1 lost wound.

note: Applies to model but official is self-repair per turn.

name: Righteous Paragons

src.description: Each time a model in this unit makes an attack that targets a MONSTER or VEHICLE unit, add 1 to the Hit roll and add 1 to the Wound roll.

note: Omits target type condition (MONSTER or VEHICLE only).

name: Rites of Castigation

src.description: In your Shooting phase, after this model has shot, select one enemy unit hit by one or more of those attacks. Until the end of the turn, each time a friendly ADEPTA SORORITAS unit makes a ranged attack that targets that enemy unit, improve the Armour Penetration characteristic of that attack by 1. The same enemy unit can only be affected by this ability once per turn.

note: Shows garbled AP modifier ('+-1'); omits scope and frequency limit.

name: Devastating Refrain

src.description: In your Shooting phase, after this model has shot, if one or more of those attacks made with an Indirect Fire weapon scored a hit against an enemy unit, that unit must take a Battle-shock test. Each time such an attack destroys an enemy model that has the Deadly Demise ability, that models Deadly Demise ability inflicts mortal wounds on a D6 roll of 5+ instead of on a 6.

note: Omits Indirect Fire weapon requirement and Deadly Demise interaction.

name: Fiery Conviction

src.description:
If this model is on the battlefield at the start of your Command phase, you can choose one of the following:

• Discard 1 Miracle dice and gain 1CP.
• Take a Leadership test for this model; if that test is passed, gain 1CP.

note: Omits both options (Miracle dice discard or leadership test).

name: Holy Mission

src.description: If this model is attached to a DOMINION SQUAD during the Declare Battle Formations step, it gains the Scouts 6"ability. If this model is attached to a SISTERS NOVITIATE SQUAD during the Declare Battle Formations step, it gains the Infiltrators ability.

note: Too vague; omits different abilities per squad type.

name: War Hymns

src.description: While this model is leading a unit, melee weapons equipped by models in that unit have the [SUSTAINED HITS 1] ability.

note: Omits that melee weapons only. Applies to all weapons.

name: Holy Piety

src.description: Each time this model makes a melee attack, unless this model’s unit is Battle-shocked, you can re-roll the Hit roll.

note: Missing condition: Battle-shocked units cannot benefit. Scope too broad.

name: Embodied Prophecy

src.description:
Each time this unit is selected to fight, select one of the following abilities to apply to melee weapons equipped by models in this unit until the end of the phase:

■ [SUSTAINED HITS 1]
■ [LETHAL HITS]

If this unit made a Charge move this turn, until the end of the phase, select both abilities above to apply to melee weapons equipped by models in this unit instead.

note: Both abilities always apply; melee only; must choose one unless charged.

name: Righteous Awareness

src.description: Once per turn, when an enemy unit ends a Normal, Advance or Fall Back move within 9"of this unit, If this unit is not within Engagement Range of one or more enemy units, it can make a Normal move of up to D6".

note: Trigger is wrong (enemy ends move, not unit selection). Range only applies to trigger check.

name: Holy Vanguard

src.description: If this unit has a Leader unit attached to it during the Declare Battle Formations step and this unit starts the battle embarked within a TRANSPORT, that Leader unit gains the Scouts 6"ability.

note: Only grants Scout 6" to attached Leader, not the unit. Timing is declaration phase.

name: Healing Tears

src.description: While this unit contains a Celestine model, in your Command phase, if this unit is below its Starting Strength, either one destroyed Geminae Superia model or up to D3 other Bodyguard models are returned to this unit.

note: Logic reverses: D3 if has Bodyguard AND no Geminae, 1 if has Geminae. Phrasing inverted.

name: Lifewards

src.description: While this unit contains one or more Geminae Superia models, Celestine has the Feel No Pain 4+ ability.

note: Applies only to Celestine model, not whole unit. Condition is Geminae presence.

name: Defenders of the Faith

src.description: At the end of your Command phase, if this unit is within range of an objective marker you control, that objective marker remains under your control until your opponent’s Level of Control over that objective marker is greater than yours at the end of a phase.

note: Ability is about objective control duration, not OC stat. Completely misdescribed.

name: Null Rod

src.description: Models in the bearer’s unit have the Feel No Pain 4+ ability against mortal wounds and Psychic Attacks.

note: Feel No Pain only applies vs mortal wounds and psychic attacks, not all damage.

name: Auto-Tapestry of the Emperor’s Judgement

src.description: While this unit is leading a unit and contains an Aestred Thurga model, weapons equipped by models in that unit have the [DEVASTATING WOUNDS] ability.

note: Requires Aestred Thurga model present. Scope narrower than generated implies.

name: Recount the Deeds of the Saints

src.description: While this unit is leading a unit and contains an Agathe Dolan model, each time that unit destroys an enemy unit, you gain 1 Miracle dice. When that Agathae Dolan model is destroyed, you gain D3 Miracle dice.

note: First trigger is unit kills while leading; unclear generation. Second trigger applies when model dies.

name: Emergency Combat Embarkation

src.description: Once per turn, in your opponent’s Charge phase, after an enemy unit has selected targets for its charge but before it makes a Charge move, you can select one Adepta Sororitas unit from your army that was selected as a target of that charge. Provided that unit is not within Engagement Range of any enemy units and every model in that unit is within 3" of this Transport, it can embark within this Transport.

note: Describes embarkation trigger as generic special, misses charge phase timing and 3" proximity requirement.

name: Holy Cover

src.description: Each time a ranged attack is allocated to a model, if that model is not fully visible to every model in the attacking unit because of this Fortification, that model has the Benefit of Cover against that attack.

note: Applies to fortification blocking, not generic shooting phase. Scoped too broadly.

name: Consecrated Ground

src.description: When this Fortification is set up, all parts of it must be set up within 1" of another part. Friendly Adepta Sororitas Infantry models can be set up or end any type of move on top of the floor sections of this Fortification. While an Adepta Sororitas unit from your army is within 1" of this Fortification, that unit can perform up to two Acts of Faith per phase, instead of just one.

note: Misses setup rule, infantry-only embarkation, and extra Acts of Faith. Describes generic stat mod.

name: Acts of Faith

src.description:
If your Army Faction is ADEPTA SORORITAS, each unit from your army with this ability can perform one Act of Faith per phase. This is done using Miracle dice.
Gaining Miracle Dice: If your Army Faction is ADEPTA SORORITAS, you gain 1 Miracle dice:

■ At the start of each battle round.
■ Each time an ADEPTA SORORITAS unit from your army is destroyed.

Each time you gain a Miracle dice, roll one D6. The number you roll is the value of that Miracle dice. This value cannot be changed or re‐rolled, unless a rule specifically states otherwise. Keep your Miracle dice to one side – this is your Miracle dice pool.
Performing an Act of Faith: Before making a dice roll for a model or unit from your army with the Acts of Faith ability, if you have one or more dice in your Miracle dice pool, that unit can perform an Act of Faith. If it does, select one of the dice from your Miracle dice pool to substitute that dice roll (if a roll involves more than one dice, e.g. a Charge roll or Battle-shock test, only a single dice can be substituted). The dice that is being substituted is not rolled; instead, the value of the selected Miracle dice is used as if it had been rolled (this counts as an unmodified dice roll of that value for all rules purposes). Each Miracle dice can only be selected for substitution once. Once all Miracle dice substitutions have been made, remove the chosen Miracle dice from your Miracle dice pool, and roll all remaining, unsubstituted dice that are apart of the dice roll. You can use Miracle dice when a unit performs an Act of Faith for any of the following types of dice roll:

■ Advance roll
■ Battle-shock test
■ Charge roll
■ Damage roll
■ Hit roll
■ Saving throw
■ Wound rollExample: Instead of rolling 2D6 to take a Battle-shock test for a unit, you could use one dice from your Miracle dice pool. If the value of that dice was 4, the unit’s Battle-shock test would be determined by rolling one D6 and adding 4 to it (i.e. it would be D6+4).

note: Generated describes -1 to hit; official is Miracle dice substitution mechanic. Completely wrong.
